key_points: Bhakti and Sufi movements emphasized devotion beyond rituals
Saints like Mirabai, Kabir, Tulsidas, Khwaja Moinuddin Chishti, and Nizamuddin Auliya played major roles
Devotion was expressed through poetry, songs, music, art, and pilgrimages
Movements encouraged social equality, tolerance, and inclusivity
Teachings influenced ethical living, service, and community harmony
examples: Kabir’s couplets taught equality and devotion to God over caste or religion
Mirabai composed poems expressing personal love for Krishna
Sufi qawwalis emphasized love, service, and spiritual connection
Communal gatherings at shrines promoted social inclusivity
exam_tips: Learn the contributions of both Bhakti and Sufi saints
Understand devotional practices and how they were expressed
Focus on inclusivity and social impact of these movements
Use examples to support answers in questions
Distinguish between Bhakti and Sufi teachings and methods
